label: The founding
headline: One chapel. Adelaide. 1922.
paragraphs: Ashley’s great-grandfather opens a small chapel on Adelaide’s east side in 1922. Twenty-three people. One hand-written sermon a week.
They called it Futures because they believed the best days of the church were still ahead. A century later, we still believe it.

label: Expansion
headline: Three more Adelaide congregations.
paragraphs: Post-war growth. Three more South Australian congregations join the Futures family across the 1950s. The Evans family takes on its second generation of pastoral leadership.
Sunday school rolls triple. Baptisms are every month. The “chapel” becomes a movement.

label: International
headline: The first US congregation opens in Phoenix.
paragraphs: Futures plants its first international campus in the US in 1987. Indonesian partnerships begin the same decade.
It’s the first time “Futures” means more than one country. The family stretches and holds.

label: Ashley & Jane
headline: The next generation takes the seat.
paragraphs: Ashley & Jane Evans become Global Senior Pastors in the year 2000. They’re young. The family is nervous. The church grows anyway.
The 2000s see Futures re-rooted in scripture, retooled for a new century, and aimed at a global horizon.

label: Indonesia
headline: Five Indonesian campuses in a decade.
paragraphs: Cemani, Solo, Samarinda, Langowan, Bali. Five campuses across Indonesia, raised up between 2008 and 2017.
Futures learns that one family can sing in more than one language, and that the best leaders are often the ones we almost missed.

label: Twenty-one and counting
headline: Futuros opens. Selah begins.
paragraphs: The Spanish-speaking Futuros campuses open in the US. Venezuela plants four at once. Selah moves from napkin sketch to prototype.
Twenty-one campuses. Four countries. One family. And the first ten years of the next hundred.

label: Today
headline: A hundred-year family, mid-stride.
paragraphs: Ashley & Jane lead a global team that spans Adelaide, Atlanta, Denpasar, and Caracas.
The vision for the next ten years is already on paper: two hundred campuses, ten thousand leaders, two hundred thousand souls.
